title: Why Plastic Pots Are Killing Your Indoor Plants
excerpt: Gemini said  Is your indoor garden struggling? The culprit might be that plastic pot. Discover why breathable terracotta is the secret to a thriving, cool, and healthy home jungle.

content.value: We have all been there. You find a gorgeous monstera or a snake plant at the nursery, bring it home, and keep it in that flimsy black plastic pot it came in. Maybe you even upgrade it to a prettier plastic planter that matches your decor. But a few weeks later, the leaves start yellowing, the soil stays soggy for days, and your plant looks like it has given up on life.
The truth is, while plastic is cheap and convenient, it is often the silent killer of indoor greenery, especially in a tropical climate like Sri Lanka.

Plants do not just breathe through their leaves; their roots need oxygen too. Plastic is non-porous. It acts like a plastic bag wrapped tightly around the root system, trapping moisture and preventing air circulation. When the roots cannot breathe, they begin to rot.

In the humid heat of Sri Lanka, water in a plastic pot has nowhere to go but down. If you overwater even slightly, that moisture sits at the bottom, creating a breeding ground for fungus and root decay. This is one of the most common reasons why indoor plants do not make it past the first few months.

Plastic is a terrible insulator. If your plant sits near a window or on a sunny balcony, the plastic absorbs the heat and cooks the roots. Think about how hot a plastic bottle gets when left in the sun. Now imagine your plant’s delicate root system sitting in that oven. In our local weather, temperatures can spike quickly, and plastic offers zero protection for the sensitive life growing inside it.
Why Terracotta Is The Boho Lifesaver
This is where the magic of earth comes in. Terracotta, literally meaning baked earth, is a living, breathing material.

At Terra by Shavindi, we believe that the vessel is just as important as the plant itself. Our handcrafted terracotta pots are naturally porous. This means they allow excess moisture to evaporate through the walls of the pot, not just the drainage hole.

This wicking action protects your plants from root rot and helps regulate the soil temperature. It keeps the roots cool even when the afternoon sun hits, mimicking the natural environment where these plants belong.

Beyond the health of your plants, there is the soul of your home. Plastic pots feel temporary and disposable. They eventually crack, fade, and end up in a landfill. They lack the character that a home truly deserves.

Terracotta, on the other hand, ages beautifully. It develops a unique patina over time that adds to that minimal, boho aesthetic we love. It is a return to heritage, using Sri Lankan clay to house your tropical plants just makes sense. It is sustainable, it is breathable, and it is what your plants actually want.
If you want your indoor jungle to actually thrive instead of just surviving, it is time to ditch the plastic. Give your plants a home that breathes and stays cool.
Explore our collection of artisanal planters at Terra by Shavindi and find the perfect, earthy match for your green friends.
